question_title: Will the community prediction be higher than 9.00% on 2026-01-14 for the Metaculus question "Will the Democrats control the House of Representatives at any point before Jan 3, 2027?"?
summary: This meta-prediction asks whether the CP for 'Democrats control House before Jan 3, 2027' will be strictly above 9.00% on Jan 14, 2026. The CP is currently at exactly 9.00% — right at the threshold. The strict inequality means staying at 9% resolves NO. The CP has been trending downward for 10 months (25% → 8-9%) and has been more often at or below 9% than above in the recent Oct-Jan period. However, the small forecaster base (22) introduces meaningful volatility, and the CP just ticked up from 8% to 9%. On balance, the strict inequality, downward trend, and recent distribution of CP values favor NO, yielding ~35% probability for YES.

factors.description: CP is currently at exactly 9.00% — the threshold. 'Higher than 9.00%' is strict inequality, so staying at 9% resolves NO. Status quo favors NO.
Long-term downward trend in CP: 25% (Mar 2025) → 8-9% (Jan 2026). Persistent downward pressure over 10 months suggests continued drift lower, not higher.
In Oct-Jan period, CP was above 9% only 26% of observations (all in early Oct at 10%). More often at or below threshold than above.
Only 22 forecasters — small base means high volatility. A single new forecaster could push CP above 9%. This adds upward uncertainty.
CP just moved UP from 8% to 9% on Jan 3, suggesting at least one forecaster recently updated upward. Could continue slightly higher.
Meta-question threshold auto-generated near current CP — structurally balanced by design. Base rate for crossing is ~50%, but strict inequality and trend favor NO side.

Key reasoning:

- **CP is exactly at threshold**: The current CP is 9.00% — the exact threshold. Since the question requires *strictly above* 9.00%, the status quo resolves NO.
- **Downward trend**: CP has steadily declined from 25% (March 2025) to 8-9% (January 2026) over 10 months.
- **Recent distribution**: In Oct-Jan, CP was above 9% only 26% of the time (all at 10% in early October). It spent 42% of the time below 9% and 32% at exactly 9%.
- **Small forecaster base (22)**: This adds volatility — one new prediction could push it above 9%, partially offsetting the downward trend.
- **Recent uptick**: CP moved from 8% back to 9% on Jan 3, showing some upward activity, but not enough to cross the strict threshold.

The strict inequality combined with the downward trend makes NO the more likely outcome, but the small forecaster count keeps YES plausible at ~35%.
